messages.content:
Which one of the following cranial nerves is responsible for the muscles of the eye?

a. Both oculomotor and abducens nerves

b. optic nerve

c. Abducens nerve

d. Oculomotor nerve
a. Both oculomotor and abducens nerves

If you have a patient who loses his touch and pressure sensation, the expected brain lobe to be affected is_______.

a. Frontal lobe

b. Occipital lobe

c. Temporal lobe

d. Parietal
d. Parietal lobe

Which one of the following muscles is innervated by cervical C2 and the accessory nerve of cranial nerves?

a. Levator scapula

b. Diaphragm

c. Sternocleidomastoid muscle

d. Trapezius Muscle
d. Trapezius Muscle

The cerebrospinal fluid circulates within sub-dura matter space and is produced by the four ventricles.

a. True

b. False

b. False

The cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) circulates within the subarachnoid space, not within the sub-dura mater space, and it is indeed produced by the choroid plexuses of the four ventricles.

Based on the structure of the cerebrum both the right and the left cerebral hemispheres are jointed in the  center by -----------?

a. Cortex

b. Cerebellum

c. corpus callosum

d. parietal lobs
c. corpus callosum

The Filum terminale is a threadlike, nonneural filament that runs from L2 and attaches to the coccyx.

a. True

b. False

b. False

The Filum terminale is indeed a threadlike, nonneural filament, but it extends from the apex of the conus medullaris (which typically ends at the level of L1-L2 in adults) down to the coccyx.

At what level of cervical nerve injury will compromise breathing and will not be able to breathe without assistance (ventilator)?

a. Both C3 and C4

b. C3

c. C4-C5

d. C4

b. C3 

Injury at the level of the C3 cervical nerve can compromise breathing to the extent that the individual may not be able to breathe without assistance (ventilator) because the phrenic nerve, which innervates the diaphragm, primarily arises from the C3, C4, and C5 nerve roots. A high cervical injury, particularly at or above C3, can impair the function of these nerves, severely affecting the ability to breathe independently. However, it's critical to understand that this is a simplified explanation; the actual impact of a spinal injury can vary based on multiple factors including the completeness of the injury.

If the musculocutaneous nerve sustained an injury, which one of the following muscles is expected to be affected?

a. Teres minor

b. biceps brachialis

c. Deltoid

d. Brachialis

e. Both biceps brachialis and brachialis

e. Both biceps brachialis and brachialis

The musculocutaneous nerve innervates the biceps brachii, brachialis, and coracobrachialis muscles in the arm, facilitating flexion of the elbow and supination of the forearm. Therefore, an injury to this nerve would affect both the biceps brachii and the brachialis muscles.

A patient sustained partial spinal cord injury that resulted in anterior horn grey matter damage, as a result of the injury he is expected to lose-------- ?

a. Motor reception

b. Sensory transmission

c. Motor transmission

 d. Sensory reception

c. Motor transmission

The anterior horn of the spinal cord's grey matter contains motor neurons that send their axons out of the spinal cord to innervate skeletal muscles. Damage to this area would impair motor transmission, leading to weakness or paralysis of muscles innervated by the affected segments of the spinal cord.

The Medial cord of the brachial plexus is formed by which one of the following?

a. posterior division of all three trunks

b. Anterior division of the middle trunk

c. Anterior division of the inferior trunk

d. Anterior division of the superior trunk

a. posterior division of all three trunks

This option is incorrect regarding the formation of the medial cord of the brachial plexus. The correct formation of the medial cord is from the anterior divisions of both the lower and middle trunks, not the posterior divisions of all three trunks. 

The correct statement regarding the medial cord of the brachial plexus should reflect its origin from the anterior divisions:

- The medial cord is actually formed by the anterior divisions of the lower (C8, T1) and middle (C7) trunks. 

There is no direct option listed that accurately represents this, but the closest in context to the correct formation—considering typical options—would be focusing on the anterior divisions rather than the posterior. Therefore, in correction to the initial prompt and options provided, it's important to note:

The medial cord of the brachial plexus is formed by the anterior divisions of the lower (C8, T1) and middle trunks (C7), not as previously misstated. Each component of the brachial plexus contributes specifically to the plexus's overall function, with the medial cord giving rise to several crucial nerves, including parts of the median nerve and the ulnar nerve.
